titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Benno C. Schmidt, Jr. Personal Papers
titlestmt.author: by Elise Riley and Mary Caldera

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 12, 2025 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Benno C. Schmidt, Jr. personal papers
did.abstract: This collection contains personal subject files kept by Benno Schmidt Jr.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 52 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Benno C. Schmidt, Jr., 2011.
Materials in the Additional subject files series were transferred from the President's Office in 1996 and Office of the Secretary in 2006.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into six series: I. Edison Schools files, 1991-2008. II. City University of New York (CUNY) files, 1995-2008. III. Office files, 1958-2009. IV. Photographs, 1996-2000. V. Computer media, 2008. VI. Additional subject files, 1986-1992.

bioghist.head: BENNO SCHMIDT JR. (1942-2023)
bioghist.p: Benno Charles Schmidt Jr. (1942-2023) '63, '66 J.D. was an education executive and constitutional law scholar. After graduating from Yale Law School, he clerked for Chief Justice of the United States Earl Warren, became a tenured professor at Columbia Law School, and was appointed dean of the school in 1984. He served as Yale's 20th president from 1986 to 1992. During his time as president, he focused on physical maintenance of campus facilities, shrinking Yale's deficits, and growing the University's endowment. After stepping down as president, he transitioned to for-profit management for public schools. From 1992 to 1997, Schmidt was the CEO for Edison Schools, later renamed EdisonLearning. He also served as chairman on the City University of New York (CUNY) Board of Trustees from 1999 to 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Benno C. Schmidt, Jr. Personal Papers (MS 1563). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

The Benno Schmidt Jr. personal papers formerly consisted of subject files transferred from the President's Office in 1996 and Office of the Secretary in 2006 (2007-m-036) now found in the "Additional subject files" series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Benno Schmidt Jr. personal papers consist of correspondence, writings, personal papers, photographs, and other documentation related to Benno Schmidt Jr.'s career and personal life.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at acquisiton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains research, clippings, correspondence, and reports related to the administration of Edison Schools Inc.

arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at acquisiton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, reports, research, and writing related to Benno Schmidt's role as chairman of CUNY Board of Trustees.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at acquisiton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, writing, legal files, and clippings related to Benno Schmidt's personal business and life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains order found at acquisiton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints and slides featuring Benno Schmidt Jr. including portraits and events photos.

did.unittitle: Photos - Benno Schmidt and Governor Engler

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: New-York Historical Society Programs / 10/2/08 - James Madison and the Constitution / 11/18/08 - The Constitution and the Age of Terror
Columbia Law School's Sesquicentennial: A Conversation with Our Deans

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains files from Yale's President's Office and the Office of the Secretary created during Benno Schmidt Jr.'s presidency.
